Stream by Sandbar

# Stream by Sandbar

## The private voice ring for everything on your mind

156 followers

## The private voice ring for everything on your mind

156 followers

Visit website

Knowledge base software

•

Wearables

•

AI Dictation Apps

Stream is a private voice ring for everything on your mind. Notes, Chat, Dictation—in one. To use Stream: just press, speak, and release. Stream can capture notes, dictate across apps, and respond to questions via earbuds or connected speakers. As a self extension, Stream supports your thinking through thoughtful questions and intelligent responses, rather than having an independent identity of its own.

results.title: Looking for Apple Dictation alternatives for Mac with better accuracy and privacy
results.content:
runs Nvidia's Parakeet TDT v3 on the Apple Neural Engine instead which is genuinely faster and handles punctuation better in my experience. The whole thing is about 2 MB, downloads the roughly 600 MB model once on first launch, and then makes zero network requests ever. The flow is push-to-talk, hold a key, speak, release, and it pastes at your cursor in whatever app you're in. No windows to manage, just sits in the menu bar. That said it does require Apple Silicon and macOS 14 or later, so if you're on an Intel machine it won't work. Sounds like Voibe is working well for you, which is great. Just useful context for anyone else landing on this thread. [...] - The suggestions in this thread are solid. Wispr Flow and the Whisper-based tools handle continuous dictation a lot better than Apple's built-in option. If on-device processing is the priority, Dictura also does that on Mac and iOS, holds a key to speak and pastes clean text at your cursor in any app, with AI cleanup that removes filler and adds punctuation automatically. Disclosure: we're the team behind Dictura, so take that into account. [...] ## Content
I have been using Apple Dictation on Mac and it is fine for quick stuff, but not great for longer dictation. It breaks flow pretty often and does not feel reliable enough when you are trying to write continuously. I am looking for something more serious for dictation. Ideally something that has really high accuracy, works fast in real time, and does not rely on cloud processing for privacy reasons. Would prefer something that runs fully on device and can keep up with natural speaking without lag. Does anything like this exist or is everyone just dealing with the same limitations? Edit: Thanks for the comments, I ended up going with Voibe.

results.title: 11 Best MacWhisper Alternatives in 2026 | Dictation, Transcription, and Free Tools
results.content:
### Should I replace MacWhisper if I mainly transcribe files?

Not necessarily. MacWhisper is already strong for files. Replace it only if live dictation, mobile writing, or a simpler free tool matters more.

Final recommendation

## Keep MacWhisper for files, choose VoiceInk for everyday writing

VoiceInk is the better first choice when MacWhisper feels too file focused and you want private live dictation on Mac and iOS. If you need free local tools, compare Hex, Handy, Amical, and Spokenly local models.

See VoiceInk pricing [...] Platforms
:   macOS and iOS

Processing
:   Local transcription by default, with optional local or user controlled AI enhancement.

VoiceInk is the cleanest fit for someone who likes the idea of SuperWhisper but wants a more affordable, open source workflow across Mac and iOS. It is built around everyday dictation into the apps you already use, with custom modes for turning rough speech into cleaner emails, chats, prompts, notes, and longer writing.

### Choose it when

 You want lifetime Mac pricing instead of a monthly bill.
 You care about local processing and open source transparency.
 You want custom modes and app aware workflows without turning dictation into a full meeting suite.

### Watch out for [...] Baseline

## What MacWhisper Already Does Well

MacWhisper is excellent when you have audio, video, interviews, meetings, podcasts, or YouTube research and need transcripts, exports, speaker workflows, or batch processing.

Switch only if your real problem is everyday voice typing, mobile dictation, voice memory, or a simpler free local tool.

Best overall Mac and iOS alternative

## VoiceInk: Best overall Mac and iOS alternative

Pricing
:   macOS: Solo $25 for 1 Mac, Personal $39 for 2 Macs, Extended $49 for 3 Macs, and Startup $159 for 10 Macs. iOS: available on the App Store.

Best for
:   Mac, iPhone, and iPad users who want local dictation, open source transparency, custom modes, and lifetime pricing on Mac.

Platforms
:   macOS and iOS
